code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or immediate return
    - Safety hazards or product failure within 30 days
    - Explicit declarations: "dangerous," "junk," "don't buy"
    - Time-sensitive failures: "Broke within X weeks"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite some positives
    - Multiple quality issues affecting daily use
    - Pattern: "Works BUT [critical flaw]..."
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with conditional utility
    - Temporary solution: "better than nothing"
    - Requires significant user effort for basic function
    - Example: "Good for occasional use but not daily"

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction without overriding recommendation
    - Functional excellence with specific minor drawbacks
    - Phrases: "Deducting one star for..." without follow-up endorsement
    - Example: "Great product but loses one star for X"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" or "highly recommend" despite critiques
    - Multi-feature praise with any drawback framed as minor
    - Transformative benefits: "life-changing," "exceeded expectations"
    - Price praise + "worth every penny" despite limitations
    - Emotional endorsement overriding specific criticisms

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Any star deduction + recommendation →5 ("Deduct 1 star but still love")
    - Explicit overall rating declarations override aspect critiques
    - Safety issues →1 regardless of other positives
    - Update sections prioritize final negative evaluation
    - Multi-aspect excellence →5 even with 1-2 minor flaws
    - Functional deficiencies requiring fixes →4 if resolved satisfactorily
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make it exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite specific critique. Strong endorsement confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: These towels look luxurious but require 3 washes to become absorbent. Expected better at this price.
<Reasoning>Aesthetic appeal countered by functional deficiency. Mixed with value concerns.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: I always hate not getting the last of jar contents. This solves it perfectly! Deducting one star because it's not dishwasher safe.
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with specific convenience drawback. No overriding endorsement →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: UPDATE: Worked great initially but broke after 2 months. Not worth the price.
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positive. Short lifespan indicates poor quality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Deducting one star for lid design, but this is revolutionary! Changed how I cook."
<Reasoning>Strong transformative praise outweighs minor critique. Emotional endorsement →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: Assembly was tricky and edges sharp. Works as intended but needs careful handling.
<Reasoning>Functional but with safety concerns. Usability issues warrant 2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: Love this organizer! Deducting one star for unclear instructions but it's worth every penny.
<Reasoning>Explicit deduction outweighed by strong value proposition →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: The towels are cute but not absorbent initially. After 3 washes, they're decent. Deducting one star.
<Reasoning>Functional deficiency requiring user effort. Satisfied with reservations →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: This blender crushes ice perfectly! Minor noise doesn't dimish its excellence.
<Reasoning>Trivial drawback mentioned as afterthought to core functionality →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Solves my problem but instructions were awful. Deducting two stars for poor documentation."
<Reasoning>Multiple star deductions without positive override indicate significant flaw →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (32 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 13
  gold 5 -> 4: 11
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 3: 1
  gold 3 -> 2: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 3, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "If there's anything you ever thought you wanted or needed in a trash can, this one has it. First, it's a semi-round, so it seems to have a smaller footprint than traditional round cans. Second, it has a silent opening and soft closing, which is great..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 32 errors, 14 borderline out of 80 examples."}
